purpose: Defines the validation RULES a checking tool should apply across this package. This is a rule definition document, not an implementation — it specifies WHAT to check, not the executable code that checks it, consistent with this project's design-vs-implementation boundary throughout.

rules.description: Every .json file in the package must parse as valid JSON.
rules.check_logic: json.load() succeeds without exception for every file.

rules.description: Every data JSON file must actually validate against its corresponding .schema.json file — not merely be syntactically valid JSON.
rules.check_logic: jsonschema.validate(instance=data, schema=schema) succeeds for every (file, schema) pair.
rules.precedent: Caught real mismatches during this project's own construction: MetroNetwork.json's white_zones_node_station lacked a 'type' field the draft schema required; BuilderRules.json's terminal pipeline step lacked a 'cross_reference' field the draft schema required. Both were schema bugs, not data bugs, and were fixed in the schema rather than retroactively editing delivered data.

rules.description: Every ID referenced across files (route IDs, station IDs, tunnel IDs, sub-location IDs, layer IDs, landmark IDs) must resolve to an actual entry in its source-of-truth file.
rules.check_logic: For every reference field, the referenced ID must exist in the set of IDs defined in its authoritative source file.
rules.precedent: Verified repeatedly during construction: RoadNetwork.json tunnel IDs against DistrictRegistry.json adjacency edges; MetroNetwork.json station/tunnel refs against RoadNetwork.json; NavigationGraph.json route/station refs against RoadNetwork.json and MetroNetwork.json; WorldPartition.json cell contents against each district's own sub_locations — the last of which caught a real gap (engineered_gardens_typology was omitted from any cell's contains list until corrected).

rules.description: Every sub-location confirmed in a district's own _data.json must be accounted for exactly once across the package's streaming cell definitions — no sub-location may be missing, and no sub-location may be double-counted as two separate physical spaces.
rules.check_logic: For each district, the union of all streaming_cells[].contains must equal that district's sub_locations set (excluding explicitly-noted content-state exceptions).
rules.precedent: Sector 0's archivist_reading_room was deliberately excluded from streaming_cells as a content-state of dead_relay_stations, not a fourth physical cell — this rule must allow explicitly-documented exceptions like this one without flagging them as errors.

rules.description: Any declared total, count, or sum field must equal the actual computed value from the data it summarizes.
rules.check_logic: declared_total == sum(actual_items) for every such field.
rules.precedent: DataLayers.json originally declared total_district_gating_layers as 29 when the actual sum of all five districts' layer arrays was 30 — a miscount on the Spire's six-layer list. Caught and corrected before delivery.

rules.description: No file in this package may modify, rename, or contradict any of the five approved district packages or Meridian_Master_Plan.md.
rules.check_logic: No write operation targets any locked_inputs path (per PackageManifest.json); checksums of locked inputs remain unchanged across the package's construction.

rules.description: No file may introduce a sixth named district with its own identity, history, or six-ownership-questions treatment.
rules.check_logic: District enumeration fields across all files contain exactly the five approved IDs: olympus_spire, white_zones, metro_archives, undercroft, sector_0. Any 'Inner Rings' or connective-tissue reference must be tagged as infrastructure/unbuilt scaffolding, never as a district.

rules.description: No file may resolve an unresolved story question, including the three flagged Open Items (Luci, Director Voss's fate, the Spire/Nyx Server Hub discrepancy).
rules.check_logic: No file contains new narrative content answering these items. Flagged items may be referenced and their status tracked, but never closed without recorded writing-team sign-off.

rules.description: The dependency graph across both the file-level and district-level tiers must be a valid DAG — no circular dependencies.
rules.check_logic: networkx.is_directed_acyclic_graph() (or equivalent) returns true for both tiers.
rules.precedent: Computationally verified for DependencyGraph.json's 29-node file-level graph at time of its own construction — confirmed DAG, not merely asserted.

rules.description: Every district-pair relationship must be documented exactly once, covering all C(5,2)=10 unique pairs with no gaps or duplicates.
rules.check_logic: The set of unordered district pairs in relationships must equal the full combinatorial set of all 5 districts taken 2 at a time.

rules.description: Recorded checksums for locked inputs must match their actual file content at any time the Builder is invoked with --strict-canon-lock.
rules.check_logic: sha256(file) == recorded_sha256 for every entry in locked_inputs.
rules.precedent: All checksums in PackageManifest.json were computed from real file content and independently spot-checked, not fabricated.

rules.description: No numeric value anywhere in this package may represent fabricated engineering precision — literal world coordinates, Unreal transforms, mesh IDs, or invented memory/performance budgets.
rules.check_logic: Manual/automated scan for coordinate-shaped or transform-shaped numeric patterns; any found value must trace to a canon-confirmed source (e.g. the ~30 million population figure) or be flagged as a violation.
rules.precedent: Verified explicitly for StreamingStrategy.json's memory_strategy section — confirmed zero fabricated MB/GB/KB values present.

rule_application_note: Rules VR-001 through VR-005 and VR-009 through VR-011 were, in practice, executed as real Python validation scripts during this package's own construction, not written after the fact as documentation of intent. VR-006 through VR-008 and VR-012 were applied as manual review discipline throughout, consistent with this project's standing practice since the first district package.
